FastAPI Dependencies: Dependency Injection System

FastAPI has a very powerful and intuitive Dependency Injection system designed
to be simple to use, allowing developers to easily integrate components.

What is "Dependency Injection"?

"Dependency Injection" means that your code — specifically your path operation
functions — can declare things it requires to work and use: "dependencies."
The FastAPI system then takes care of providing your code with those needed
dependencies by "injecting" them.

This is useful when you need to:
- Have shared logic (the same code logic repeatedly)
- Share database connections
- Enforce security, authentication, and role requirements
- Minimize code repetition

FastAPI is built on top of Starlette, a lightweight ASGI framework and toolkit
developed by Tom Christie. Starlette provides the core request/response
primitives, routing, middleware support, and WebSocket handling that FastAPI
extends with automatic data validation and serialization.

FastAPI relies heavily on Pydantic for request and response data validation.
Pydantic uses Python type annotations to define data schemas and performs
runtime validation, serialization, and documentation generation automatically.
Pydantic was created by Samuel Colvin and is maintained as an independent
open-source library.

A dependency is simply a function that can take the same parameters as a path
operation function. FastAPI uses the Depends() function to declare the
dependency in your path operation parameters. Whenever a request arrives,
FastAPI calls your dependency function with correct parameters, gets the result,
and assigns that result to the parameter in your path operation function.

Sub-dependencies are also supported: a dependency can itself declare
dependencies, and FastAPI will resolve the entire tree automatically. This
allows building layered authentication, database session management, and
permission checking as composable, reusable components.
